### Post by Jad on 2006-06-20
on same desktop I once installed Fedora5 and I was able to use resolutions higher than 1024, now with Dapper I can't. I have nvidia glx installed but still cannot select resolution higher than 1024 and actually on System->Preferences->Screen Resolution only 640/800 and 1024 appears despite I added more resolutions to the xorg.conf
Please advise.


---

### Post by tommcd on 2006-06-21
Did you use the graphical install CD? For some reason the graphical CD does not have an option to choose screen resolutions. The alternate CD does.
You can do:
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
this will allow you choose screen resolutions at the end of reconfiguring xorg. Then just reboot and you should have your desired screen resolutions. You will need to reinstall nvidia driver after this.
Hope this helps.

---

### Post by Jad on 2006-06-22
Thank you dude, 
it worked but now I have a kind of stretch menues and icons
